description.current: Audit WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across 4 layers (Lighthouse, keyboard nav, screen reader, contrast). Use before public release, after UI changes, or on a11y complaints.
issues: Terse description lacks actionable trigger phrases—"WCAG audit", "check a11y", "keyboard navigation test", "screen reader validation" not present in description itself
Description is imperative ("Use before...") but doesn't explain value: no mention that audit catches issues automated tools miss

issues.description: Bloated preamble (lines 13-33): 3 paragraphs + diagram before first actionable step; could be collapsed to 2 sentences + reference the diagram
Rigid WCAG scoring thresholds (lines 186-191) presented as rules without explanation WHY these specific weights (30% auto, 25% keyboard, 25% screen reader, 20% visual). No rationale for why 85 = PASS
Keyboard test protocol (lines 69-77) is exhaustive but includes 8 steps—no prioritization. 'Keyboard traps' (step 7) should be flagged as CRITICAL override early
Layer 3 (Screen Reader) checks use abstract checklist format; no instructions on HOW to use accessibility tree snapshots to verify each check

strengths: Clear 4-layer structure with diagram—immediately frames scope
Layer 1 (Lighthouse) and Layer 2 (Keyboard) have concrete, runnable steps with artifact names
Evidence capture template (lines 91-100) is practical—exact filenames given
Dark mode audit is thoughtful inclusion (lines 157-170) — often forgotten
Final report template uses concrete field names (lines 196-226) — verdicts writer can copy directly

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: Keyboard test protocol (lines 69-77) + evidence capture steps (lines 81-88) repeat tab/screenshot/record pattern that could be a shell script automating snapshot capture into e2e-evidence/accessibility/keyboard/ with sequential naming

top_fixes: Rewrite description (3-4 sentences max): lead with WCAG 2.1 AA, add 3 trigger phrases ('WCAG audit', 'keyboard navigation test', 'screen reader validation'), explain it catches issues Lighthouse misses (keyboard traps, ARIA misuse). Target 40-50 words.

Move scoring rules (lines 178-191) to a bundled reference/scoring-rules.md; replace with: 'Weighted across 4 layers (see references/scoring-rules.md). Final verdict: >85=PASS, 70-85=CONDITIONAL (HIGH issues documented), <70=FAIL. **Override: any keyboard trap = FAIL regardless of score.**'

Collapse preamble (lines 13-33): replace with single sentence: 'Systematic audit covering automated scans, keyboard navigation, screen reader structure, and visual accessibility. Each layer scored and weighted for final verdict.'
Add subsection to Layer 3: 'How to verify each check using accessibility tree' — show 1 concrete example (e.g., 'For check 3.1, count <h1> elements in the tree snapshot; 0 or >1 = FAIL')

Move keyboard test steps 1-8 to a reference/keyboard-test-protocol.md; keep only the 8-item list here with note: 'Run in order. **CRITICAL: Step 7 (keyboard trap) triggers automatic FAIL regardless of score.'
Dark mode audit is good — keep as is

test_prompts.prompt: I need to audit our React dashboard before launch next week. It's a data viz app with interactive charts, form inputs, and a dark mode toggle. Some users complained they couldn't navigate with keyboard only. How do I run a full WCAG AA audit including keyboard navigation to catch all accessibility issues?
test_prompts.expected_output: Clear guidance to run 4-layer audit (Lighthouse via DevTools, tab through all controls, check a11y tree for labels/roles, verify 4.5:1 contrast). Specific evidence paths. Verdict template that says PASS or FAIL with numbered issues.

test_prompts.prompt: After our refactor, I need to prove we didn't break accessibility. We have Lighthouse at 85 before the change. What baseline evidence do I capture, and how do I detect regressions in keyboard nav or screen reader experience?
test_prompts.expected_output: Capture baseline: Lighthouse JSON, keyboard test findings table (lines 91-100 format), a11y tree snapshot. After refactor: re-run same 4 layers, compare side-by-side. Any keyboard navigation that worked before but fails now = REGRESSION = FAIL.

test_prompts.prompt: Our designer added new form fields with custom aria-label attributes. How do I verify the ARIA is correct and won't confuse screen readers? Do I need to test with an actual screen reader?
test_prompts.expected_output: Use accessibility tree snapshot (Chrome DevTools MCP) to inspect a11y tree structure. Check Layer 3 items 3.5-3.8: inputs have labels, ARIA roles match behavior, ARIA states update. No actual screen reader needed for this audit — tree snapshot is sufficient. Findings go in step-03-screen-reader.md.
